# Ryno Tools

> Ryno Tools is a free, offline-capable microlearning platform for certification exam prep and language learning.
> Last updated: 2026-06-11. No login, no paywall — all content is freely accessible.
> Study ham radio (Technician, General, Extra class), falconry apprentice licensing,
> CompTIA Security+ SY0-701, and DevOps through adaptive flashcard quizzes and timed exam simulations.
> Learn Japanese through a structured curriculum following Japan's 6-3-3 school system.
> No login required. Works offline as a Progressive Web App.

## Quizzes Available

<!-- quizzes:start -->
- [Technician](https://ryno.tools/ham-radio/): 409+ questions — Entry-level FCC license covering regulations, operating procedures, and electronics fundamentals. Required for VHF/UHF band access. Typical study time: 10–20 hours.
- [General](https://ryno.tools/ham-radio/): 456+ questions — Intermediate license that unlocks HF band privileges. Covers propagation, antenna theory, advanced operating procedures, and FCC regulations. Typical study time: 20–30 hours.
- [Extra](https://ryno.tools/ham-radio/): 706+ questions — Top-tier FCC license covering advanced regulations, EMI, transmission theory, and expanded privileges. Typical study time: 40–60 hours.
- [Apprentice](https://ryno.tools/falconry/): 398+ questions — Preparation for the falconry apprentice exam required in all 50 U.S. states. Covers raptor biology, mews housing, health and nutrition, training, and federal/state hunting regulations (50 CFR Part 21).
- [Security+ SY0-701](https://ryno.tools/security-plus/): 100+ questions — 100 practice questions aligned to CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 objectives: threats and vulnerabilities, cryptography, access control, network security, incident response, and governance and compliance.
- [Core Concepts & Culture](https://ryno.tools/devops/): 50+ questions — 50 questions covering DevOps culture, CI/CD principles, SRE and reliability engineering (error budgets, SLI/SLO/SLA), Agile and Lean fundamentals, and DORA metrics — sourced from Google SRE Book, Accelerate, Team Topologies, and the Agile Manifesto.
- [Containers & Kubernetes](https://ryno.tools/devops/): 53+ questions — 53 questions covering container fundamentals, image layers and registries, Kubernetes pods and workloads, services and networking, storage (PV/PVC), and troubleshooting (CrashLoopBackOff, OOMKilled, ImagePullBackOff) — sourced from kubernetes.io and docs.docker.com.
- [Linux Fundamentals](https://ryno.tools/devops/): 50+ questions — 50 questions covering process management, filesystems and storage, users and permissions, networking basics, and shell and text processing — sourced from Linux man pages, kernel.org, Red Hat docs, and the Filesystem Hierarchy Standard.
- [Networking for DevOps](https://ryno.tools/devops/): 50+ questions — 50 questions covering DNS and name resolution, TCP/IP and routing, TLS and certificate management, load balancers and proxies, and HTTP and application protocols — sourced from RFCs, NGINX docs, Cloudflare Learning Center, and MDN.
- [CI/CD Pipelines](https://ryno.tools/devops/): 50+ questions — 50 questions covering pipeline fundamentals, GitHub Actions, Jenkins and GitLab CI, testing and quality gates, and deployment strategies (blue/green, canary, rolling, feature flags) — sourced from GitHub Actions docs, GitLab CI docs, Jenkins docs, and Humble & Farley's Continuous Delivery.
- [Infrastructure as Code](https://ryno.tools/devops/): 50+ questions — 50 questions covering Terraform core concepts and state management, Ansible fundamentals and advanced patterns, and IaC principles including Pulumi, GitOps, immutable infrastructure, and policy-as-code — sourced from Terraform docs, Ansible docs, Pulumi docs, and Kief Morris's Infrastructure as Code.
- [Cloud Platforms](https://ryno.tools/devops/): 50+ questions — 50 questions covering cloud fundamentals, AWS core services (EC2, S3, IAM, RDS/DynamoDB), GCP and Azure patterns, networking and security (VPC, WAF, KMS), and architecture patterns (HA, DR, RPO/RTO, serverless) — sourced from AWS, GCP, and Azure Well-Architected Frameworks.
- [Monitoring & Observability](https://ryno.tools/devops/): 50+ questions — 50 questions covering metrics and dashboards (Prometheus, Grafana), logging (structured logs, ELK, Loki), distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry, Jaeger), alerting and on-call practices, and reliability concepts (SLI/SLO/SLA, error budgets, four golden signals) — sourced from OpenTelemetry spec, Google SRE Book, and Prometheus docs.
<!-- quizzes:end -->

## Japanese Learning

Hub: https://ryno.tools/japanese/

Structured curriculum following Japan's 6-3-3 school system (13 path nodes: 1 Foundations + 6 Elementary + 3 Junior High + 3 High School). Currently available:

- Foundations: Hiragana (104 characters with vivid mnemonics, gojūon grid), Katakana (104 characters with loanword examples)
- Elementary 1: Kanji Grade 1 (80 kyōiku kanji), Vocabulary I (24 greetings/colors/everyday words), Numbers & Counters (50 items: 1–10,000, counters 人/本/枚, time, days of week), Sentences I (40 N5 grammar patterns: AはBです, question forms, 6 core particles は/が/を/に/へ/で, これ/それ/あれ demonstratives)

Coming soon: Elementary 2–6 (1,026 total elementary kanji), Junior High 1–3 (full 2,136 jōyō kanji set, keigo honorific system, passive/causative), High School 1–3 (N2/N1 grammar, classical 古文, Sino-Japanese 漢文 kanbun).

Three active-recall modes: Learn (flashcard), Match (multiple-choice), Type (romaji input). Mastery-gated progression. Free, no login, offline-capable PWA.

## Features

- Tag-based question filtering
- Timed exam mode with pass/fail scoring
- Instant feedback with explanations
- Light/dark mode
- Installable as a PWA (works offline)

## Articles

<!-- blog-articles:start -->
- [How to Learn Hiragana: The Complete Beginner's Guide](https://ryno.tools/blog/how-to-learn-hiragana/) — Hiragana is the essential first step in Japanese. Learn the 46-character gojūon grid, understand the full 104-character set, and use active recall to read hiragana in 1–2 weeks.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-11
  track: japanese/foundations
  tags: hiragana, kana, beginners, mnemonics, study-tips
- [Japanese Particles Explained for Beginners: は, が, を, に, へ, で](https://ryno.tools/blog/japanese-particles-guide/) — Particles are the glue of every Japanese sentence — but は is pronounced 'wa', を is pronounced 'o', and が vs は confuses everyone. This guide breaks all six essential N5 particles down clearly, one at a time.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-11
  track: japanese/grammar
  tags: particles, grammar, wa-ga, n5, beginners
- [What Is JLPT N5? The Beginner's Entry Point to Japanese Proficiency](https://ryno.tools/blog/what-is-jlpt-n5/) — JLPT N5 is the entry-level Japanese proficiency certification — roughly 800 vocabulary words, ~100 kanji, and basic grammar. Here's what the test looks like, how it's scored, and whether it belongs on your study plan.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-11
  track: japanese/foundations
  tags: jlpt, n5, japanese-proficiency, grammar, beginners
- [What Is CI/CD? A Beginner's Guide to Continuous Integration and Delivery](https://ryno.tools/blog/what-is-cicd-beginners-guide/) — Every DevOps interview asks about CI/CD. Here's what it actually means: how continuous integration differs from continuous delivery, what a pipeline does, and why trunk-based development beats long-lived branches.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-09
  track: devops/core-concepts
  tags: cicd, automation, culture, git
- [What Is an SLI, SLO, and SLA? A Plain-English Guide for DevOps Beginners](https://ryno.tools/blog/what-is-sli-slo-sla-devops/) — SLI, SLO, and SLA sound similar but play very different roles. Learn what each one means, how error budgets connect them, and why high-performing teams use them to balance reliability and velocity.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-09
  track: devops/core-concepts
  tags: reliability, observability, culture
- [Falconry Mews & Housing Standards Every Apprentice Must Know](https://ryno.tools/blog/falconry-mews-housing-standards/) — A mews is more than a shed with a bird in it. Falconry housing standards are federally regulated and heavily tested on the apprentice exam. Here's what you need to know.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-08
  track: falconry/apprentice
  tags: Equipment, Rules and Regulations, Health
- [Ohm's Law Made Simple for the Ham Radio Technician Exam](https://ryno.tools/blog/ohms-law-technician-exam/) — Ohm's Law — V = IR — appears on every Technician license exam. Master the formula, the units, and the common exam scenarios in one focused read.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-08
  track: ham-radio/technician
  tags: voltage, resistance, current, power
- [Symmetric vs. Asymmetric Encryption: A Security+ SY0-701 Primer](https://ryno.tools/blog/symmetric-vs-asymmetric-encryption/) — Symmetric encryption uses one key; asymmetric uses two. That distinction drives dozens of Security+ SY0-701 exam questions on TLS, key exchange, digital signatures, and PKI.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-08
  track: cybersecurity/sy0-701
  tags: cryptography, network security
- [Can You Take the Ham Radio Exam Online in 2026?](https://ryno.tools/blog/ham-radio-exam-online-2026/) — Remote ham radio exams have been available since 2020 and are now a mainstream option. Here is everything you need to know to take your Technician exam online.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-07
  track: ham-radio/technician
  tags: exam-prep, online-exam, technician, ve-session
- [How Hard Is the Ham Radio Technician Exam?](https://ryno.tools/blog/how-hard-is-ham-radio-technician-exam/) — Most people pass the ham radio Technician exam on their first try with 10–20 hours of study. Learn the question count, passing score, topic breakdown, and best study methods.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-07
  track: ham-radio/technician
  tags: exam-prep, technician, study-tips, fcc
- [How Many Questions Are on the Falconry Apprentice Exam?](https://ryno.tools/blog/how-many-questions-falconry-apprentice-exam/) — The falconry apprentice exam is typically 100 questions and requires an 80% score to pass. Here is what the test covers, how scoring works, and how to register through your state wildlife agency.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-07
  track: falconry/apprentice
  tags: exam-prep, apprentice, falconry-license, regulations
